generated_oml:
// C3: Physical Sensing Components
instance CranePositionEncoder : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Optical encoder measuring joint angles for crane kinematic state estimation. Provides high-resolution position feedback for all crane axes."
    DTDFVocab:producedData CranePositionData
]

instance CraneForceTorqueSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Six-axis force/torque sensor mounted at the crane's end-effector to measure payload forces and moments during operation."
    DTDFVocab:producedData CraneForceTorqueData
]

instance CraneActuatorCurrentSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Current sensors monitoring actuator motor currents to detect overload conditions and energy consumption patterns."
    DTDFVocab:producedData ActuatorCurrentData
]

instance CraneVelocitySensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Velocity encoders providing real-time velocity feedback for all crane axes to enable closed-loop control."
    DTDFVocab:producedData CraneVelocityData
]

instance CraneTrajectoryLogger : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "System logging actual trajectory execution including timestamps, position/velocity profiles, and residual swinging motion."
    DTDFVocab:producedData TrajectoryExecutionData
]

// C4: Physical-to-Virtual Interaction
instance CranePositionData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom CranePositionEncoder
]

instance CraneForceTorqueData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom CraneForceTorqueSensor
]

instance ActuatorCurrentData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom CraneActuatorCurrentSensor
]

instance CraneVelocityData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom CraneVelocitySensor
]

instance TrajectoryExecutionData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom CraneTrajectoryLogger
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance KinematicModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Python-implemented kinematic model of the gantry crane using gantrylib library, capable of forward/inverse kinematics and trajectory generation."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo PythonRuntimeEnvironment
]

instance TrajectoryOptimizationModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Real-time trajectory optimization model minimizing energy consumption while ensuring collision avoidance and kinematic feasibility."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo PythonRuntimeEnvironment
]

instance FaultDetectionModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Machine learning-based fault detection model analyzing sensor data patterns for abnormal vibration spectra and current spikes."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo PythonRuntimeEnvironment
]

instance HistoricalOperationalData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Time-series storage of operational metrics including joint positions, payload forces, actuator currents with versioning support."
    DTDFVocab:fromData CranePositionData, CraneForceTorqueData, ActuatorCurrentData, TrajectoryExecutionData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo TimescaleDB
]

instance RealTimeCraneStateData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Current state data of the crane including position, velocity, acceleration, and payload status for real-time synchronization."
    DTDFVocab:fromData CranePositionData, CraneVelocityData, TrajectoryExecutionData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo MQTTBroker
]

instance SimulatedTrajectoryData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Predicted trajectory data generated by the kinematic model for comparison with actual execution."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ContinuousValidationService
]

instance FaultDiagnosisData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Processed sensor data with anomaly flags and root cause analysis for detected faults."
    DTDFVocab:fromData CraneForceTorqueData, ActuatorCurrentData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo FaultDetectionService
]

// C11: Enablers
instance PythonRuntimeEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Python execution environment hosting all crane control logic, kinematic modeling, and optimization algorithms via gantrylib library."
    DTDFVocab:enables TrajectoryOptimizationService, FaultDetectionService, ContinuousValidationService
]

instance DockerContainer : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Containerized deployment of Digital Twin services ensuring reproducibility and service isolation."
]

instance TimescaleDB : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Time-series database optimized for high write/read throughput of crane operational metrics with SQL compatibility."
    DTDFVocab:enables DataLoggingService
]

instance InfluxDBCompatibilityLayer : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Compatibility layer enabling data exchange with open-source InfluxDB for scalability."
]

instance MQTTBroker : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Eclipse Mosquitto MQTT broker facilitating real-time data streaming between physical sensors and Digital Twin services."
    DTDFVocab:enables DataLoggingService, ContinuousValidationService, FaultDetectionService
]

instance GrafanaDashboard : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Interactive dashboard platform for real-time and retrospective visualization of crane states and performance metrics."
]

instance RESTAPI : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "RESTful API interface for service-to-service interactions including control command distribution."
    DTDFVocab:enables ControlService, TrajectoryOptimizationService
]

instance GitVersionControl : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Git-based version control system managing Digital Twin code and model evolution."
]

// C6: Services
instance TrajectoryOptimizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Real-time trajectory optimization service minimizing energy consumption while ensuring collision avoidance through kinematic model integration."
    DTDFVocab:provides OptimalTrajectoryInsight, ControlParameterAdjustmentAction
]

instance DataLoggingService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Time-series data storage and versioning service for operational metrics with compatibility to open-source databases."
    DTDFVocab:provides HistoricalDataInsight, DataVersioningAction
]

instance VisualizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Real-time and retrospective visualization of crane states including 3D CAD synchronization and performance dashboards."
    DTDFVocab:provides CraneStateVisualizationInsight, PerformanceMonitoringAction
]

instance ContinuousValidationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Real-time synchronization service detecting discrepancies between physical crane and digital twin with automated comparison capabilities."
    DTDFVocab:provides TwinAccuracyInsight, AnomalyFlaggingAction
]

instance FaultDetectionService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Proactive fault diagnosis service using pattern recognition in sensor data for predictive maintenance."
    DTDFVocab:provides FaultDiagnosisInsight, PredictiveMaintenanceAction
]

instance ControlService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Closed-loop control service executing optimized trajectories with dynamic parameter adjustment based on physical feedback."
    DTDFVocab:provides ControlExecutionInsight, ParameterAdjustmentAction
]

instance SystemEvolutionService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Formalized representation of crane system evolution including hardware/software upgrades and model refinements."
    DTDFVocab:provides SystemChangeInsight, VersionControlAction
]

// C17: Insights/Actions
instance OptimalTrajectoryInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Optimized trajectory paths minimizing energy consumption while respecting kinematic constraints and collision avoidance requirements."
]

instance ControlParameterAdjustmentAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Dynamic adjustment of control parameters based on real-time feedback from the physical system."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance HistoricalDataInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Long-term performance trends and degradation patterns extracted from operational metrics stored in time-series database."
]

instance DataVersioningAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automated versioning of operational data to support historical analysis and model validation."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance CraneStateVisualizationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Synchronized 3D visualization of crane states including end-effector position, joint angles, and payload status."
]

instance PerformanceMonitoringAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Real-time monitoring of key performance indicators including cycle time, energy use, and fault occurrences."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance TwinAccuracyInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [

]

instance AnomalyFlaggingAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automated flagging of deviations exceeding predefined thresholds (±5% error margin)."

]

instance FaultDiagnosisInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [

instance PredictiveMaintenanceAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Proactive maintenance recommendations based on fault detection patterns and historical data trends."

]

instance ControlExecutionInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Execution status of optimized trajectories with real-time feedback on performance and constraints compliance."
]

instance ParameterAdjustmentAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Dynamic tuning of control parameters to maintain system stability and performance."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance SystemChangeInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Impact analysis of system modifications including new sensors, hardware upgrades, and model refinements."
]

    base:desc "Version-controlled management of crane models and digital twin components."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "The **Virtual-to-Physical Interaction** in the Digital Twin system is mediated by the **Control Application** and **Trajectory Generator Service**, which translate digital commands into physical actions for the **Physical Crane**. Key components include:
  - **Command Types**:
    - **Trajectory commands**: Generated by the **Trajectory Generator Service** using a **kinematics model** to produce **optimal movement trajectories** (e.g., spline-based paths) with **zero residual swinging motion** at termination.
    - **Configuration commands**: Sent via the **Human Machine Interface (HMI)** to adjust crane parameters (e.g., speed limits, safety zones).
  - **Control Message Formats**: Implicitly structured as **Python objects** or **serialized data** (e.g., JSON/Protobuf) transmitted via the **Physical Crane Interface** to the crane’s controllers.
  - **Validation and Authentication**:
    - **Trajectory Validation**: The **Continuous Validation Service** ensures executed trajectories match simulations by comparing **distance metrics** (e.g., positional error) against thresholds. If breached, operators are notified via the **Dashboard**.
    - **Command Authentication**: Not explicitly detailed, but inferred as **implicit trust** within the closed-loop system (no mention of cryptographic mechanisms).
  - **Feedback Systems**:
    - **Real-time sensor feedback** from the **Physical Crane** (e.g., encoders, IMUs) is looped back to the **Logging Application** and **Validation Service** for continuous monitoring.
    - **Operator feedback**: The **HMI** provides visual/auditory alerts for threshold breaches or system anomalies.
  - **Conflict Resolution**:
    - **Priority-based**: Critical safety commands (e.g., emergency stops) likely override trajectory commands, though specifics are undocumented.
    - **Trajectory Replanning**: The **Trajectory Generator Service** may recompute paths if simulations predict collisions or instability.
  - **Command Execution Confirmation**:
    - Implicit via the **Logging Application**, which records executed trajectories alongside timestamps. The **Validation Service** cross-checks these logs against simulations to confirm adherence to commands."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin systems in the documents adopt a **lightweight, ad-hoc orchestration approach** rather than relying on commercial frameworks. **GreenhouseDT** uses a **hybrid architecture** combining **InfluxDB (time-series database)**, **ActiveMQ (message broker)**, and **virtual machines (VMs)** for behavioral adaptation, with VMs preloaded with prerecorded data to simulate real-time synchronization. The **Air Quality Management** exemplar employs a **Microsoft Azure-based pipeline** for runtime visualization, simulation, and prediction, integrating **AutomationML** for model-driven digital twin engineering. Both exemplars emphasize **containerization/virtualization** (e.g., VMs, Raspberry Pi) for modular deployment but lack explicit orchestration patterns like microservices or edge-cloud coordination. The **gantry crane** system, while inspired by cyber-physical logistics, is described using **C4 Model** for architecture visualization but provides no details on orchestration mechanisms. **No mention of resource management, service mesh, or dynamic reconfiguration** is present."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "The *GreenhouseDT* exemplar emphasizes **behavioral adaptation** of both the physical system and its digital twin, implying dynamic fidelity adjustments to maintain alignment. Validation methods are not explicitly described, but the use of **prerecorded data** in virtual machine images suggests offline testing and comparison against historical or simulated scenarios. The *Air Quality Management* exemplar focuses on **runtime visualization, simulation, and prediction**, indicating a reliance on model-driven validation via Azure tools, though no formal verification methods (e.g., uncertainty quantification, error correction) are detailed. Uncertainty sources (e.g., sensor noise, model approximations) are not quantified, and no explicit quality assurance processes are documented. The ad-hoc approach in both exemplars implies manual or domain-specific validation rather than standardized protocols."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "The **MQTT Broker** serves as the central communication hub for inter-service signaling, enabling asynchronous message exchange between containers (e.g., **Trajectory Generator Service**, **Simulation Service**, **Logging Application**). The system is deployed on a **personal computer**, implying a localized network topology with minimal latency. No specific communication protocols (e.g., TCP/IP, UDP) or network architectures (e.g., star, mesh) are detailed in the documents. Bandwidth and latency requirements are not quantified, but the real-time nature of trajectory validation suggests low-latency constraints. Redundancy mechanisms are not mentioned, nor are security protocols (e.g., encryption, access control) for network communication. The **TimescaleDB database** and **Grafana Dashboard** rely on the same infrastructure, with no explicit separation of network segments."
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system is deployed **on-premise** on a **personal computer**, with all software containers (e.g., **Trajectory Generator Service**, **Simulation Service**, **Logging Application**) running locally. The architecture supports a **monolithic deployment model** via a **docker compose file**, consolidating services into a single host environment. Computational requirements are tailored to the **TimescaleDB database** (for time-series data storage) and the **Trajectory Generator Service** (for real-time kinematics calculations), with no explicit scalability mechanisms (e.g., horizontal/vertical scaling) described. Storage is managed within the database, with no mention of external cloud or edge hosting. Availability and reliability are not quantified, but the system’s design implies high dependability on the local hardware and network stability. No automatic failover or backup mechanisms are referenced."
]

instance horizontal_integration : DTDFVocab:HoriIntegration [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system integrates horizontally via **MQTT (Message Queuing Telemetry Transport)** for lightweight, publish-subscribe communication between the physical gantry crane and its digital counterpart. No enterprise-wide interoperability standards (e.g., OPC UA, Industry 4.0 protocols) or dependencies on external Digital Twin frameworks are documented. The system’s integration is confined to internal containerized services, with no explicit APIs or gateways for third-party systems. Data exchange is limited to the MQTT broker, which acts as the sole intermediary for sensor/actuator data."
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "The **Air Quality Management** exemplar adheres to **AutomationML** for digital twin modeling, a **standardized engineering format** for automation and control systems, ensuring interoperability with industry tools. The **GreenhouseDT** exemplar uses **InfluxDB** (a de facto standard for time-series data) and **ActiveMQ** (a widely adopted message broker protocol), though neither is a domain-specific digital twin standard. The **lab-scale gantry crane** system employs the **C4 Model** for architectural documentation, a **software-centric modeling standard** rather than a digital twin-specific framework. No **certification requirements** or **compliance frameworks** (e.g., ISO 23247 for digital twins) are mentioned, and interoperability is limited to **ad-hoc integrations** (e.g., AutomationML, InfluxDB/ActiveMQ stacks) rather than cross-domain standards."
]
